name: Tim Cook
executiveSummary:
Tim Cook is an American business executive who has been the Chief Executive Officer of Apple Inc. since 2011. Under Cook's leadership, Apple has expanded its product line, deeply integrated services into its ecosystem, and become the first publicly traded U.S. company to reach a $3 trillion market capitalization.

Cook is known for his operational mastery, transforming Apple's supply chain into one of the most efficient in the world. He has also been a vocal advocate for user privacy, environmental sustainability, and human rights.
biography:
Cook grew up in Robertsdale, Alabama. He earned a Bachelor of Science in industrial engineering from Auburn University in 1982, and his Master of Business Administration from Duke University's Fuqua School of Business in 1988.

Following graduation, Cook spent 12 years in IBM's personal computer business, ultimately serving as the director of North American fulfillment. He later served as Chief Operating Officer of the computer reseller division of Intelligent Electronics and was Vice President for Corporate Materials at Compaq for six months.

Steve Jobs hired Cook to join Apple in 1998 as Senior Vice President for Worldwide Operations. He was later promoted to Chief Operating Officer. When Jobs stepped down due to health reasons in 2011, Cook was named CEO. During his tenure, Apple has released the Apple Watch, AirPods, and the Apple Vision Pro, alongside massively growing its Services revenue.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Industrial Engineering

recentNews.title: Apple Intelligence Launch
recentNews.description: Spearheaded Apple's massive push into generative AI with 'Apple Intelligence' across iOS and macOS.

recentNews.title: Vision Pro Launch
recentNews.description: Oversaw the release of the Apple Vision Pro, entering the spatial computing market.
